heading: Definitions
label: 3501 Definitions
notes: References in TextThe Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act, referred to in par. (2)(C), is , , , which is classified generally to chapter 33 (§ 1601 et seq.) of Title 43, Public Lands. L. 115–325, § 1132 Stat. 4445Dec. 18, 2018“This Act [enacting sections 167 and 3507 of this title, amending this section, sections 415, 3115a, 3502 to 3504, and 3506 of this title, , Conservation, and , The Public Health and Welfare, and enacting provisions set out as notes under sections 3104 and 3504 of this title and ] may be cited as the ‘Indian Tribal Energy Development and Self-Determination Act Amendments of 2017’.”section 800 of Title 16section 6863 of Title 42section 800 of Title 16

children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Office of Indian Energy Policy and Programs, Department of Energy.

children.label: A
children.text: any land located within the boundaries of an Indian reservation, pueblo, or rancheria;

children.label: i
children.text: in trust by the United States for the benefit of an Indian tribe or an individual Indian;

children.label: ii
children.text: by an Indian tribe or an individual Indian, subject to restriction against alienation under laws of the United States; or

children.label: iii
children.text: by a dependent Indian community;

children.label: C
children.text: land that is owned by an Indian tribe and was conveyed by the United States to a Native Corporation pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ( et seq.), or that was conveyed by the United States to a Native Corporation in exchange for such land;43 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: D
children.text: any land located in a census tract in which the majority of residents are Natives (as defined in section 3(b) of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ()); and43 U.S.C. 1602(b)

children.label: E
children.text: any land located in a census tract in which the majority of residents are persons who are enrolled members of a federally recognized Tribe or village.

children.label: A
children.text: an Indian reservation in existence in any State or States as of ;August 8, 2005

children.label: B
children.text: a public domain Indian allotment; and

children.label: i
children.text: on original or acquired territory of the community; or

children.label: ii
children.text: within or outside the boundaries of any State or States.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().25 U.S.C. 450b1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: For the purpose of paragraph (12) and sections 3503(b)(1)(C) and 3504 of this title, the term “Indian tribe” does not include any Native Corporation.

The term “integration of energy resources” means any project or activity that promotes the location and operation of a facility (including any pipeline, gathering system, transportation system or facility, or electric transmission or distribution facility) on or near Indian land to process, refine, generate electricity from, or otherwise develop energy resources on, Indian land.
The term “Native Corporation” has the meaning given the term in section 3 of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ().43 U.S.C. 1602
The term “organization” means a partnership, joint venture, limited liability company, or other unincorporated association or entity that is established to develop Indian energy resources.
The term “Program” means the Indian energy resource development program established under .section 3502(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: carried out a contract or compact under title I or IV of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.) for a period of not less than 3 consecutive years ending on the date on which the Indian tribe submits the application without material audit exception (or without any material audit exceptions that were not corrected within the 3-year period) relating to the management of tribal land or natural resources; or25 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: B
children.text: substantial experience in the administration, review, or evaluation of energy resource leases or agreements or has otherwise substantially participated in the administration, management, or development of energy resources located on the tribal land of the Indian tribe.
The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

The term “sequestration” means the long-term separation, isolation, or removal of greenhouse gases from the atmosphere, including through a biological or geologic method such as reforestation or an underground reservoir.

children.label: A
children.text: any enterprise, partnership, consortium, corporation, or other type of business organization that is engaged in the development of energy resources and is wholly owned by an Indian tribe (including an organization incorporated pursuant to or ); andsection 5124 of this titlesection 5203 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any organization of two or more entities, at least one of which is an Indian tribe, that has the written consent of the governing bodies of all Indian tribes participating in the organization to apply for a grant, loan, or other assistance under or to enter into a lease or business agreement with, or acquire a right-of-way from, an Indian tribe pursuant to subsection (a)(2)(A)(ii) or (b)(2)(B) of .section 3502 of this titlesection 3504 of this title

The term “tribal land” means any land or interests in land owned by any Indian tribe, title to which is held in trust by the United States, or is subject to a restriction against alienation under laws of the United States.
